persona: First-time EdgeWell user; Node.js developer; 20-minute budget
summary: Two blocker-class doc/code-drift issues: README quickstart invocation (node bin/edgewell.js ...) fails on every example because the legacy bin/edgewell.js still imports the deleted src/index.js; and the README's documented npm install fails on the link: protocol. Additional high-severity bugs: a profile set --key value corruption bug, and the README's exact RAG example query returns no matches. Several medium-severity UX issues (--version dumps help; unknown command dumps full help; raw ENOENT/EISDIR errors leak from rag ingest; self-test runs 0 tests; version-history fails in a git checkout; vector dim inconsistent between doctor and vector stats; redact is uninvokable; seed count is off by ~20% on journal).

findings.title: README quickstart does not work — node bin/edgewell.js <cmd> fails with ERR_MODULE_NOT_FOUND for every documented example
findings.repro: Follow README step 1: `node bin/edgewell.js help`. Same for all 8 README quickstart commands.
findings.expected: Help screen (or the appropriate command output)
findings.actual: Error [ERR_MODULE_NOT_FOUND]: Cannot find module '.../src/index.js' imported from .../bin/edgewell.ts — 8/8 README examples fail
findings.suggestedFix: Delete the legacy `bin/edgewell.js` (or update it to forward to `bin/edgewell.ts`), and update the README to use `pnpm start -- <cmd>` (matching the actual `scripts.start` invocation) or `node dist/bin/edgewell.js <cmd>` (matching the actual `bin` field).

findings.title: README's exact RAG example query returns no matches
findings.repro: `node --import tsx/esm bin/edgewell.js rag ingest data/sample_health_notes.txt` then `rag search "how much water should I drink"`
findings.expected: At least one matching chunk (the file contains the line 'Hydration: 2 liters of water a day supports kidney function.')
findings.actual: (no matches). Single-word queries like `rag search water` do return the relevant chunk, so the issue is with the multi-word query.
findings.suggestedFix: Inspect the TF-IDF query parser — likely a stop-word / tokenization mismatch for multi-word queries. Verify with the README's exact query string.

findings.title: README install command `npm install` fails; project requires pnpm
findings.repro: Follow README Install section: `npm install`
findings.expected: A working install
findings.actual: `npm error EUNSUPPORTEDPROTOCOL: Unsupported URL Type "link:"` for `@qvac/sdk` (which is `link:./vendor/qvac-sdk`) plus `npm warn Unknown project config "onlyBuiltDependencies"` for the `.npmrc` key that's valid for pnpm but not npm.
findings.suggestedFix: Update README to use `pnpm install` (or `corepack enable && pnpm install`) as the install command. The package.json pins `packageManager: pnpm@11.6.0`, so the install instructions must match.

findings.title: `profile set --key value` corrupts profile.json with leftover argv as keys
findings.repro: `edgewell profile set --name alex --goal sleep` (or any `--key value` invocation)
findings.expected: Reject with a clear error: `usage: edgewell profile set <key> <value> (positional args only)`
findings.actual: Persists `"--name": "alex --goal sleep"` as a real top-level key in data/profile.json, sets `createdAt`, and returns `set --name` as the success message.
findings.suggestedFix: In the `profile set` handler, reject any argv starting with `-` before consuming positional args, and add a unit test for the `--key value` case to lock the fix in.

findings.title: `--version` flag silently ignored — prints the entire help screen
findings.repro: `edgewell --version`
findings.expected: `edgewell v3.0.0` (matches the `edgewell version` subcommand output)
findings.actual: Full 110-line help screen, exit 0
findings.suggestedFix: Handle `--version` / `-V` in the argument parser before the help path — print the version and exit 0.

findings.title: `profile init` hangs silently when stdin is not a TTY
findings.repro: Run `edgewell profile init` in a non-interactive shell (e.g. CI)
findings.expected: Either create a default profile, or error: `profile init requires a TTY — use \`profile set <key> <value>\` for non-interactive setup`
findings.actual: Hangs at the `name [friend]:` readline prompt, then exits 0 with no profile.json written
findings.suggestedFix: Detect `!process.stdin.isTTY` and either fall through to a default profile or print a clear error and exit non-zero.

findings.title: `doctor` falsely reports `profile readable name=friend` when data/profile.json is missing
findings.repro: Delete `data/profile.json`, run `edgewell doctor`
findings.expected: `WARN  no profile on disk — run \`edgewell profile init\``
findings.actual: `OK    profile readable  name=friend, lang=en` — the check is actually reading an in-memory default, not the on-disk file
findings.suggestedFix: Check the file's existence (or `existsSync`) before the readability probe, and report the actual on-disk state.

findings.title: Inconsistent vector dimensions reported: doctor says 64, vector stats says 128
findings.repro: Run `edgewell doctor` then `edgewell vector stats` back-to-back on the same data
findings.expected: Same dim in both
findings.actual: `dim=64` from doctor vs `dim: 128` from vector stats
findings.suggestedFix: Resolve the dim to a single source of truth (config or constant) and have both commands read from it. Likely one path is reading the constructor default and the other is reading the runtime value.

findings.title: `redact` command cannot be invoked from the CLI
findings.repro: `edgewell redact "Contact me at john@example.com"` and `edgewell redact --text "Contact me at john@example.com"`
findings.expected: Redacted text in both cases
findings.actual: Both invocations return `usage: edgewell redact <text|json> <...>`
findings.suggestedFix: Either implement the positional-text path, or update the usage to spell out the expected invocation (e.g. `redact <text|json> [more chunks...]`). Add a worked example to the help screen.

findings.title: `self-test` reports 'self-test passed' while running 0 tests
findings.repro: `edgewell self-test`
findings.expected: Run the project's test suite and report a real pass/fail count
findings.actual: `tests 0, suites 0, pass 0, fail 0, ... self-test passed`
findings.suggestedFix: Wire `self-test` to `child_process.spawnSync('node', ['--test', 'test/*.test.ts'])` (or the equivalent for the compiled `test/*.test.js`); or rename to `self-check` if it really is just an internal smoke probe, and have it actually exercise something.

findings.title: `version-history` says 'not a git checkout or CHANGELOG not tracked' inside a git checkout with a tracked CHANGELOG.md
findings.repro: Run `edgewell version-history` from the repo root (which is a git repo, with CHANGELOG.md tracked)
findings.expected: List of recent CHANGELOG.md sections / commits
findings.actual: `not a git checkout or CHANGELOG not tracked`
findings.suggestedFix: Fix the git/CHANGELOG detection in the `version-history` handler — likely a path resolution issue (relative to CWD vs. `import.meta.url`). Add a regression test that runs from the repo root.

findings.title: `seed N` consistently undercounts journal entries by ~20%
findings.repro: `edgewell seed 10` → reports 8 journal entries; `edgewell seed 10000` → reports 9001 journal entries (expense count is correct in both cases)
findings.expected: `seeded 10 journal entries and 10 expenses`
findings.actual: `seeded 8 journal entries and 10 expenses` (and the same ~20% gap at scale)
findings.suggestedFix: Inspect the journal-loop in the seed handler — likely an off-by-one in a `for` bound or a category-fork that skips some iterations. Add a unit test: `assert(seed(10).journals === 10)`.

findings.title: Unknown subcommand dumps the entire 110-line help screen
findings.repro: `edgewell frobnicate`
findings.expected:
`unknown command: frobnicate

Did you mean: ...

Run \`edgewell help\` for the full list.`
findings.actual: Full 110-line help screen, exit 2
findings.suggestedFix: Catch the unknown-command case before the help-path branch and print a one-line error with a `Did you mean:` suggestion (use Levenshtein over the known command list).

findings.title: Raw `ENOENT` / `EISDIR` errors leak from Node's `fs` layer for rag ingest and multimodal
findings.repro: `rag ingest /tmp/nope.txt` and `rag ingest data` (a directory)
findings.expected: `file not found: /tmp/nope.txt` and `data is a directory, not a file` — matching the friendly style already used by `import` and `compare`
findings.actual: `error: ENOENT: no such file or directory, open '...'` and `error: EISDIR: illegal operation on a directory, read`
findings.suggestedFix: Wrap `fs.readFile` calls in `rag ingest` and `multimodal` in a try/catch that maps `ENOENT` → `file not found:` and `EISDIR` → `not a file:`. The `import` and `compare` handlers already do this; lift that helper into a shared module.

findings.title: `seed` accepts arbitrarily large N with no cap, no progress, no warning
findings.repro: `edgewell seed 1000000`
findings.expected: Either cap at a reasonable number (e.g. 100 000) and warn, or print a progress line every 1 000 rows, or both
findings.actual: Hung for >60 s with no output and no progress indicator before being killed
findings.suggestedFix: Add a soft cap (e.g. `if (n > 100000) { console.warn('... capping at 100000'); n = 100000; }`) and a `process.stdout.write` progress tick every 1 000 rows.

findings.title: `chat` only processes one piped input then exits cleanly — no multi-line REPL for scripts
findings.repro: `printf "hi\nexit\n" | edgewell chat`
findings.expected: Process both `hi` and `exit`, or read until EOF and process all lines
findings.actual: Only `hi` is processed; the second `you>` prompt prints, then EOF exits 0
findings.suggestedFix: If `!process.stdin.isTTY`, read all of stdin (e.g. via `readline` accumulating `line` events until `close`) and process each line; honour `exit`/`quit` as a graceful stop.

findings.title: `expense add 250` (no category) silently defaults to `'other'` with no warning
findings.repro: `edgewell expense add 250`
findings.expected: Error: `missing category — usage: edgewell expense add <amount> <category>`
findings.actual: `logged 250 (other)` — quietly mis-tagging data
findings.suggestedFix: Add a positional-arg-count check in the `expense add` handler: if `args.length < 1`, print usage and exit 2.

findings.title: `version` and `version-history` disagree on what counts as a release
findings.repro: Run `edgewell version` (prints `v3.0.0`) and `edgewell version-history` (prints `not a git checkout or CHANGELOG not tracked`)
findings.expected: Coherent narrative — at minimum, `version-history` should be able to find the v3.0.0 entry in CHANGELOG.md
findings.actual: `v3.0.0` exists; the changelog-history walker can't find it (see UAT-FN-11 for the underlying git-detection bug)
findings.suggestedFix: Fix the underlying git-detection bug (UAT-FN-11); this finding will resolve as a side effect.

findings.title: Help screen lists 60+ top-level commands plus a 138-command wall of name-only 'additional commands'
findings.repro: Run `edgewell help` and scroll to the bottom
findings.expected: 8–12 top-level commands with descriptions; advanced commands in `edgewell command-list` only
findings.actual: 60+ described top-level commands + 138 additional commands dumped as a name-only wall on the first paint of help
findings.suggestedFix: Move the `tag-*`, `journal-entries-*`, `expenses-entries-*` families behind `edgewell command-list` (or group them under a single `tag <sub>` / `journal <sub>` namespace) so the default help screen is scannable.

findings.title: `where` shows `profile: data/profile.json` even when the file does not exist
findings.repro: Delete `data/profile.json`; run `edgewell where`
findings.expected: `profile: (not yet created — run \`edgewell profile init\`)`
findings.actual: `profile: /…/data/profile.json` (same line as when the file exists)
findings.suggestedFix: In the `where` handler, check existence for each listed path and append a `(not yet created)` marker when missing — same as `size` already does (it shows `0 B`).
